featureName: BUG-021-011: migrate the four prior LLM-judgment evaluators onto the agent.InvokeJudgment foundation
reporter: owner directive (best solution long term — complete the foundation)

executionHistory.summary: BUG-021-010 introduced agent.InvokeJudgment[T] and proved it on the new hospitality evaluator, but the four pre-existing evaluators (cooling, alert_timing, resurface_eval, expertise_eval) still each carried a private xBridgeRunner interface, an ErrXEvaluatorUnavailable sentinel, and a ~30-line hand-rolled marshal→invoke→validate→decode body — four copies of the transport the foundation centralizes. The owner's 'best solution long term' directive calls to converge them. Confirmed (grep) the four sentinels + four interfaces are referenced only inside each evaluator file and its test (no producers, no wiring), so removal is contained.

Single behaviour-preserving bugfix-fastlane scope: retype each BridgeXEvaluator.Runner to agent.JudgmentRunner; delete the four xBridgeRunner interfaces and the four ErrXEvaluatorUnavailable sentinels; replace each method body with one agent.InvokeJudgment[T] call (expertise unwraps resp.Classifications and keeps its empty-topics short-circuit); keep the nil-receiver guard (returns agent.ErrJudgmentUnavailable); drop the now-unused fmt import; update the four test nil assertions to agent.ErrJudgmentUnavailable. No new tests — the existing evaluator tests are the regression guard. Rejected the sentinel-alias alternative (keeps four redundant names; diverges from hospitality). SCN-021-MIGRATE-01..02.

cooling.go / alert_timing.go / resurface_eval.go: Runner -> agent.JudgmentRunner; sentinel + interface removed; body -> if b == nil { return zero, agent.ErrJudgmentUnavailable }; return agent.InvokeJudgment[Decision](ctx, b.Runner, "scheduler", scenarioID, in). expertise_eval.go: same, source "api", InvokeJudgment[expertiseResponse] then return resp.Classifications; kept expertiseRequest/expertiseResponse + len(topics)==0 short-circuit. Dropped fmt import from all four (json/errors remain for init() no-op tool). Updated the four test nil-receiver/nil-runner assertions to agent.ErrJudgmentUnavailable and corrected the stale 'coolingBridgeRunner' comment in cooling_test.go.

go build ./... + vet green (unused fmt would have failed the build). All four evaluators' existing tests pass: TestBridge{Cooling,AlertTiming,Resurface,Expertise}Evaluator_* (parse, routing, signal forwarding, internal-field non-leak, nil-receiver/nil-runner now on agent.ErrJudgmentUnavailable, all error paths). TestInvokeJudgment_* (the shared primitive) green.

Full intelligence + agent + digest + cmd/core + scenario-lint packages green. The production wiring (BridgeXEvaluator{Runner: bridge}) compiles against the retyped agent.JudgmentRunner field because *agent.Bridge satisfies it. The test scripted runners satisfy agent.JudgmentRunner (same Invoke signature). No producer/scheduler test affected (no behaviour change).

This phase IS the simplification: net ~135 fewer lines (179 deletions, 44 insertions) across the eight files — four interfaces, four sentinels, and four ~30-line transport bodies collapse to four one-line InvokeJudgment calls. The codebase now has exactly one judgment transport.

The nil-receiver guard is retained in each method (a method on a nil *BridgeXEvaluator must not dereference b.Runner); the nil-runner path is handled inside InvokeJudgment. Both yield agent.ErrJudgmentUnavailable, so the producers' graceful-degradation paths (gate on the error / nil evaluator) are unchanged. expertise keeps its empty-topics short-circuit.

No change to what is sent to the LLM: each evaluator still marshals the same signals struct (internal fields remain json:"-"; the non-leak tests still pass). InvokeJudgment performs no payload logging. No new external surface. No framework files touched.
artifact-lint PASSED for the packet; build + vet + affected packages green. Evidence in report.md.

grep of the four removed interface names + four removed sentinel names across internal/ and cmd/ = 0 (fully removed). No migration; no scenario YAML / SST / wiring / producer / schema change; diff confined to the four evaluator files + four tests; no .github/bubbles changes; parent spec 021 planning artifacts untouched.

Each migrated evaluator's struct doc-comment now notes the shared agent.InvokeJudgment transport (BUG-021-010). The packet records continuation of BUG-021-010 and the convergence to one judgment primitive across the six BUG-021-005..010 LLM-driven judgments.

scopeProgress.title: Migrate the four evaluators onto agent.InvokeJudgment

lockdownState.reason: behaviour-preserving refactor; no convergence loop required

resolvedFindings.summary: The four pre-existing LLM-judgment evaluators (cooling, alert timing, resurface, expertise) still each carried a private xBridgeRunner interface, an ErrXEvaluatorUnavailable sentinel, and a hand-rolled copy of the marshal/invoke/validate/decode transport that BUG-021-010 centralized into agent.InvokeJudgment[T].
resolvedFindings.resolution: Migrated all four onto agent.InvokeJudgment: Runner retyped to agent.JudgmentRunner, the four interfaces + four sentinels removed, each body replaced by one InvokeJudgment call (expertise unwraps resp.Classifications), the unused fmt import dropped. Behaviour preserved — all existing evaluator unit tests pass; ~135 fewer lines.
resolvedFindings.evidence: report.md Test/Code-Diff/Audit Evidence; internal/intelligence/{cooling,alert_timing,resurface_eval,expertise_eval}.go; grep of the removed identifiers = 0.
